subject: Psychology
grade_subject_fit: Source-derived Cycle A package using fictional examples, teacher/student separation, tiered work, decision plus reason, and safe classroom boundaries.
unit_context: Cycle A A6 examines emotion, motivation, and stress responses using evidence, context, and limits.
assignment_purpose: Students explain a fictional case by connecting emotion, motivation, body response, context, coping choice, and limits.
final_evidence_target: A support-plan case analysis with claim, evidence, decision plus reason, coping strategy, and limit.

student_task_flow.label: Core concepts
student_task_flow.evidence: Students define emotion, motivation, need, incentive, stressor, appraisal, coping, arousal, resilience, context.

student_task_flow.label: Fictional case evidence
student_task_flow.evidence: Students separate emotion, motivation, stressor, body response, and context.

student_task_flow.label: Decision plus reason
student_task_flow.evidence: Students choose a coping or support strategy and justify it.

success_criteria: I can distinguish emotion, motivation, and stress.
I can use fictional case evidence.
I can choose a support strategy and explain why.
I can avoid personal disclosure or diagnosis.
differentiation_model: Supported students identify emotion, stressor, and support from a labelled case. Proficient students choose and justify a support strategy. Extending students compare two supports and explain fit, limit, and possible unintended effect.
checkpoint_release_logic: Students complete case evidence table before final support-plan response.
teacher_implementation_notes: Do not ask students to disclose personal stress, family concerns, health information, or private experiences.
Keep support strategies general, school-appropriate, and non-clinical.
Use decision plus reason for major tasks.
likely_misconceptions: Emotion and behaviour are the same thing.
One coping strategy works for everyone.
pacing_shape: Five-part package: vocabulary, emotion and motivation, stress appraisal, coping choices, final support-plan case analysis.

teacher_guide.title: Cycle A A6 Teacher Guide — Emotion, Motivation, and Stress
teacher_guide.overview: Students use fictional cases to explain emotion, motivation, and stress responses. The package avoids forced personal disclosure and keeps support strategies general and school-appropriate.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How can we explain emotion, motivation, and stress without reducing people to labels?
teacher_guide.big_idea: Responses are shaped by appraisal, body response, needs, incentives, coping options, and context.
teacher_guide.opening_frame: A careful explanation separates what the character feels, what motivates them, what stressor is present, what evidence we have, and what support may fit.
teacher_guide.frame_line: A6 frame: feeling, need, stressor, evidence, support, limit.
teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Final response should identify case evidence, choose a support strategy, give a reason, and include a limit.
learning_goals: Define emotion, motivation, stressor, appraisal, and coping.
Choose a support strategy and justify it.
Include a limit and avoid diagnosis.
integrity_checks: No ranking students by stress or coping.
Use fictional or general cases only.
teacher_notes: If students personalize the case, redirect to fictional evidence.
If students propose advice, ask what evidence supports it.
If students choose a support, require why this support fits the case.
For extension, students compare two supports and identify limits.
questions: What need or goal may matter?
look_fors: Student chooses a support with reason.

timing.activity: Introduce emotion, motivation, and stress vocabulary.
Analyze fictional emotion and motivation cases.
Study stress appraisal and body response.

model.title: Support-plan model
steps: Claim: the character may be appraising the task as high pressure.
Evidence: the case shows avoidance, time pressure, and performance concern.
Support: break the task into smaller steps and add a check-in point.
Reason: this reduces uncertainty and supports action.
Limit: the case does not show every cause or a clinical need.

bullets: Stress: response to demands and appraisal.

content.scenario: A fictional student avoids starting a large project after several deadlines overlap.

content.model: Choose a support that fits the evidence, not generic advice.

task_sheet.title: A6 Student Packet — Emotion, Motivation, and Stress
task_sheet.purpose_line: Use fictional case evidence to choose and justify a support strategy.
instructions: Make a decision and give a reason.

tasks.label: Vocabulary organizer
tasks.prompt: Define emotion, motivation, need, incentive, stressor, appraisal, coping, arousal, resilience, context.

tasks.label: Supported pathway
tasks.prompt: Use the case table to identify emotion, need, stressor, evidence, and possible support.

tasks.label: Proficient pathway
tasks.prompt: Choose the best support for the fictional case. Give evidence and a limit.

tasks.label: Extending pathway
tasks.prompt: Compare two support strategies and decide which fits better. Explain possible limits or unintended effects.

tasks.label: Final assessment
tasks.prompt: Write a support-plan case analysis using feeling, need, stressor, evidence, support, reason, and limit.

embedded_supports: The support I choose is... because...

exit_ticket.title: A6 Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: Choose a support for a fictional stress case. Give one reason and one limit.
